title: sour velvety croatian potatoes hash
description: energizing breakfast dish featuring floury potatoes, deglaze, and a hint of sourness. traditionally cooked in scallop shells, this recipe is gluten-free-friendly.
instructions: heat 1 tablespoon butter in a large skillet over medium heat. add diced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and golden brown, about 8 minutes. add minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly to avoid burning. add diced potatoes and stir to coat with butter and onion mixture. cook, stirring occasionally, for 12-15 minutes or until potatoes are tender and golden brown. add remaining 1 tablespoon butter, broken into small pieces, and stir to combine. add sour cream and stir to combine. add apple cider vinegar and stir to deglaze, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. season with paprika, salt, and pepper to taste. serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.

cook the potatoes in a large scallop shell or frying pan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 15-20 minutes or until tender and golden brown. add the remaining ingredients and stir to combine. cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the mixture is heated through. season with salt and pepper to taste. serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
